text:
There sat a man named Oliver, staring out the window at the grey day. He pondered whether to call his wife or go for a walk. After some reflection, he decided to climb onto his motorcycle and ride through the dreary town, maybe catching some sunlight.

As he pedaled through the streets, he noticed something peculiar. Birds suddenly took to the skies like nothing he'd ever seen—a band of them flying erratically, singing in what seemed like a weird polyphony.

Oliver blinked. Was it his imagination? He followed the birds closely. They headed towards the mountains. He got on his bike and followed, the melody growing more intricate. That evening, as he rested under a leafy tree, he listened to the birds sing his favorite love song. The melody reminded him of her.

He wondered how he'd ever communicate his feelings. Maybe he should give her a poem.

He wrote it on paper, then sat back listening to the silence of the night.
